paragraphs_list.text: The Glory of God in His Goodness to Israel
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. A Song.

verse_parts.text: Let those also who hate Him flee before Him.
As wax melts before the fire,
let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
But let the righteous be glad;
Sing to God, sing praises to His name;
MT deserts; Tg. heavens (cf. v. 34 and Is. 19:1)
Lit. Lord, a shortened Heb. form
A father of the fatherless, a defender of widows,
God sets the solitary in families;
He brings out those who are bound into prosperity;
But the rebellious dwell in a dry
O God, when You went out before Your people,
When You marched through the wilderness,
at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
You, O God, sent a plentiful rain,
You, O God, provided from Your goodness for the poor.
And she who remains at home divides the
Though you lie down among the
like the wings of a dove covered with silver,
And her feathers with yellow gold.”
When the Almighty scattered kings in it,
fume with envy, you mountains of
You have received gifts among men,
But God will wound the head of His enemies,
The hairy scalp of the one who still goes on in his trespasses.
The Lord said, “I will bring back from Bashan,
back from the depths of the sea,
LXX, Syr., Tg., Vg. you may dip your foot
And the tongues of your dogs
The procession of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.
The singers went before, the players on instruments
The Lord, from the fountain of Israel.
LXX, Syr., Tg., Vg. Command, O God
Your God has commanded your strength;
Strengthen, O God, what You have done for us.
Because of Your temple at Jerusalem,
Kings will bring presents to You.
Rebuke the beasts of the reeds,
The herd of bulls with the calves of the peoples,
submits himself with pieces of silver.
Envoys will come out of Egypt;
Ethiopia will quickly stretch out her hands to God.
Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth;
Oh, sing praises to the Lord,
To Him who rides on the heaven of heavens,
Indeed, He sends out His voice, a mighty voice.
more awesome than Your holy places.
